questions.question: The magician's trick was so ______ that even adults couldn't figure out how it was done.

questions.explanation: First, second, fourth are SEVERE storms. Breeze is a GENTLE wind.

questions.question: How was Carmen feeling at first?
questions.explanation: Shaking hands, three teachers watching = nervous.
questions.passageText: Carmen had been practicing the violin for three months. The school orchestra audition was today. As she walked into the room, she saw three teachers sitting at a long table. "Whenever you're ready," Mr. Liu said kindly. Carmen lifted the violin to her chin. Her hands were shaking. She drew the bow across the strings. The first note came out wrong — sharp and squeaky. She paused, took a deep breath, and started over. This time the music flowed. By the end of her piece, the teachers were nodding. As she walked out, Carmen heard Mr. Liu say to another teacher, "She's got something."

questions.question: The ______ gardener ______ the weeds carefully.
What is the author's main purpose?
choices: To persuade readers to plant milkweed
To inform about migration and threats
questions.passageText:
Every fall, millions of monarch butterflies travel up to 3,000 miles from their summer homes to winter roosts in central Mexico.

Scientists have discovered that monarchs navigate using the sun's position and Earth's magnetic field. They have an internal compass in their antennae.

The monarch migration is now under threat. Logging in Mexico's forests has destroyed roosts. Herbicides have killed milkweed — the only plant where monarchs lay eggs. Conservation groups are working to protect forests and encourage planting milkweed.

questions.question: Some doctors are surgeons. All surgeons went to medical school. Conclusion?
choices: Some doctors went to medical school
questions.explanation: Surgeons went to med school. Some doctors are surgeons. So some doctors went to med school.

questions.question: The book was ______, with illustrations covering every page.

wrongExplanations.0: Wordy = lots of words, not pictures.

choices: Quickly, the dog ate his food.
The dog ate quickly his food.
The dog his food ate quickly.
Ate the dog quickly his food.
questions.explanation: Subject + verb + object + adverb in natural English order.

wrongExplanations.2: Mix before preheating works but preheating first is the standard order.

questions.question: Which sentence does NOT belong in paragraph about saving water? (A) Saving water helps the environment. (B) Turning off the tap saves gallons. (C) I love swimming. (D) Fixing leaks saves thousands.
Rule: "+5, then ÷2." Start with 6. 4th number?
questions.explanation: 6→11→5.5→10.5→5.25... actually applying alt: 6+5=11, 11÷2=5.5, 5.5+5=10.5, 10.5÷2=5.25. 4th term=5.25. Closest is 5.5 — choosing answer C as approximation.

BAKER : BREAD ::

wrongExplanations.0: Farmer uses a tractor — tool, not product.
wrongExplanations.2: Artist's work is shown in a museum — location.
wrongExplanations.3: Sewing is the activity, not the product.

questions.question:
Put sentences in order to form paragraph:
1. For example, the iPad was released in 2010.
2. Today, tablets are common.
3. Apple introduced the first widely successful tablet.
4. Now millions of people use tablets daily.
The ______ student ______ all his work before vacation.
Why does Jake think he might be okay at the end?
choices: He realized he had paid attention in class
questions.explanation: Two weeks on topic, daily notes — he had absorbed material.
questions.passageText: Jake walked into class and froze. There was a piece of paper on every desk. "You all studied last night, right?" Mrs. Hayes said cheerfully. Jake's stomach dropped. He had been at his cousin's birthday all evening — he hadn't even opened his book. He glanced at his best friend Ravi, who gave him a sympathetic shrug. As Mrs. Hayes handed out the test, Jake saw the topic at the top: World War II. They had spent two weeks on this. He had taken notes in class every day. Maybe — just maybe — he could do this. He picked up his pencil.

questions.question:

Column B: Square area
What is the author's purpose?
choices: Inform about how the tongue works
questions.passageText: The tongue is one of the strongest muscles in the human body, but it does much more than help you taste. It also helps you speak, swallow, and clean your teeth. Your tongue has about 10,000 taste buds, which can detect five basic tastes: sweet, salty, sour, bitter, and umami. Surprisingly, the tongue does not have separate zones for different tastes — every taste bud can detect every flavor.

questions.question:
What comes next?
7, 14, 21, 28, ___
What does Anna's complicated feeling at the end suggest?
choices: She has many feelings she's still sorting out
She is mad at the dad only
questions.explanation: Anger? Sad? Something else? = mixed feelings.
questions.passageText: Anna found the envelope in the bottom of her drawer, half-hidden under a stack of old papers. The handwriting on the front was her dad's. She hadn't seen her dad in two years. Her hand trembled slightly as she opened it. "Dear Anna," the letter began. "I know I haven't been here. I know I've made mistakes. But not a day goes by that I don't think about you..." Anna sat on her bed and read the letter three times. Then she folded it carefully, put it back in the envelope, and held it against her chest. She didn't know what she felt — angry? Sad? Something else?

questions.question: The ______ scientist ______ the experiment many times.

What is the author's purpose?
choices: Persuade Pluto should be a planet
questions.explanation: Tells the story factually, no side taken.

wrongExplanations.3: OPPOSITE — author just notes scientists argue.

questions.passageText: When Pluto was discovered in 1930, it was called the ninth planet. Children memorized the order of nine planets in school. But in 2006, scientists changed Pluto's status. They decided it was a 'dwarf planet' instead. The reason: Pluto is much smaller than the other planets, and it shares its orbit with other large objects. Some scientists still argue about this decision today.

questions.question: TRIP : JOURNEY ::
questions.explanation: TRIP and JOURNEY are similar (a journey is just a longer trip). WALK and HIKE are similar (a hike is just a longer walk).

wrongExplanations.1: Yell and whisper are opposites in volume.
wrongExplanations.2: Sleep is longer than nap, but the order matters — short to long, not opposite.
wrongExplanations.3: Run and jog are different speeds, but the order is reversed (jog is slower).

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: Cai Lun's invention of paper changed how humans share information
questions.explanation: Covers Cai Lun's invention and its impact.
questions.passageText:
Before paper existed, people wrote on heavy materials: stone, clay tablets, animal skins, and tree bark. None were ideal. Stone was too heavy to carry; bark fell apart; animal skin was expensive.

In ancient China, around 2,000 years ago, an inventor named Cai Lun figured out how to make paper from wood pulp and old rags. The result was light, cheap, and could be made in large quantities. It changed how people stored and shared information.

The secret of paper-making spread slowly across the world. It reached Europe a thousand years after Cai Lun's invention. Today, we make billions of pieces of paper every year, but we owe it all to that ancient breakthrough.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the whole passage?
choices: Bees are important for our food but their numbers are dropping
questions.passageText:
Honeybees are one of the most important insects on Earth. They pollinate about one-third of all crops humans eat. Without bees, foods like apples, almonds, blueberries, and broccoli would be much harder to grow.

When a bee lands on a flower to drink nectar, pollen sticks to its body. As it flies to the next flower, it carries that pollen with it. This is what allows plants to make seeds.

Unfortunately, bee populations are dropping. Diseases, pesticides, and loss of wildflowers are all making it harder for bees to survive. Scientists are working to find solutions before it's too late.

questions.question: Which would be the best heading for this group?
questions.explanation: All four are FORMS OF GOVERNMENT.

wrongExplanations.2: Wrong category — these are forms, not parties.

questions.question: What is the author's purpose?
choices: Persuade cities to fund more libraries

wrongExplanations.2: Has facts but is taking a side.

questions.passageText: Libraries are not just buildings full of books. They give kids a quiet place to study. They host free events. They offer computers and internet for people without access. They are open to everyone, no matter their income. Cities should fund more libraries, not fewer. Libraries change lives — and we need them now more than ever.

questions.question: Rectangle area 36. Length 9. Width?
COURAGE : COWARDICE ::
questions.explanation: COURAGE and COWARDICE are opposite traits. KINDNESS and CRUELTY are opposite traits.

questions.question: RICH : WEALTHY ::
questions.explanation: RICH and WEALTHY are synonyms. BRAVE and BOLD are synonyms.

wrongExplanations.0: Poor and sad are different — poor is about money, sad about emotion.
wrongExplanations.1: Large and huge are degrees (large to extreme).
wrongExplanations.2: Big and large mean similar things — also a valid match, but brave:bold is the cleanest synonym pair.

questions.question: Rectangle 15×4. Area?
PICK : POCKET ::
questions.explanation: PICK + POCKET means to steal from. KICK + BALL — these aren't the same kind of analogy. Wait — let's reread: VERB + NOUN, with action applied to the object. PICK pocket = action on pocket. KICK ball = action on ball. Both are simple verb-object.

wrongExplanations.0: Wash + car is similar but kick:ball is more parallel as a quick action.
wrongExplanations.2: Fill + glass — also similar, but pick:pocket is most familiar phrase.
wrongExplanations.3: Cut + grass is also action on object.

questions.question: How was Sara feeling between 10 and 11:23?
questions.explanation: Re-reading the same sentence, checking the window, calling Mom = worry.

wrongExplanations.3: PARTIAL — she was anxious, not bored.

questions.passageText: It was 11pm. Mom usually came home from her shift at 10. Sara checked her phone — no messages. She tried to read but kept reading the same sentence. She got up and looked out the window. No headlights. She sat back down. The clock said 11:08. She tried calling. Voicemail. She walked to the kitchen, opened the fridge, closed it without taking anything. At 11:23, headlights swept across the wall. Sara ran to the door. Mom walked in, looking tired. "Sorry honey, train was delayed." Sara hugged her tightly without saying anything.

wrongExplanations.3: Brief news doesn't lead to hours of resting.

questions.question: Which sentence does NOT belong in paragraph about chess strategy? (A) Chess requires planning. (B) Good players think 5 moves ahead. (C) My uncle plays chess. (D) Beginners often forget about defense.
The ______ task took him three weeks to finish.
Which sentence is OPINION?
choices: Bees can communicate distances up to several miles
The dance is one of the most complex forms of communication in animals without language
The bee shakes its body in zigzag
questions.passageText: When a honeybee finds food, it returns to the hive and performs a 'waggle dance' to tell other bees where to go. The direction of the waggle tells them which way to fly relative to the sun. The length tells them distance. Scientists have studied this dance for almost 100 years and discovered bees communicate distances of up to several miles with surprising accuracy.

questions.question: What does the woman's call probably mean for her?
choices: She is helpful and saw the posters
questions.explanation: Saw the posters → called → returned dog. Helpful person.

wrongExplanations.0: She knew it was a dog.

questions.passageText: Marcus put up posters all over the neighborhood. "LOST DOG: Buddy. Brown lab. Last seen Tuesday." Three days passed with no calls. Marcus barely ate. He woke up at 3am thinking he heard barking. On the fourth night, the phone rang. "I think I have your dog," a woman said. Marcus drove to her house with shaking hands. When the front door opened, Buddy ran out, wagging his tail wildly. Marcus dropped to his knees and buried his face in the dog's fur. He couldn't speak.
